subcommands.name: read
subcommands.description: reads data from Vault at the given path

subcommands.name: write
subcommands.description: writes data from Vault at the given path

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: deletes secrets and configuration from Vault at the given path

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists data from Vault at the given path

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: authenticates users or machines to Vault using the provided arguments

subcommands.name: agent
subcommands.description: this command starts a Vault agent that can perform automatic authentication in certain environments

subcommands.name: server
subcommands.description: this command starts a Vault server that responds to API requests. By default, Vault will start in a 'sealed' state. The Vault cluster must be initialized before

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: prints the current state of Vault including whether it is sealed and if HA mode is enabled. This command prints regardless of whether the Vault is sealed

subcommands.name: unwrap
subcommands.description: unwraps a wrapped secret from Vault by the given token. The result is the same as the 'vault read' operation on the non-wrapped secret. If no token is given, th

subcommands.name: audit
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for interacting with Vault's audit devices. Users can list, enable, and disable audit devices

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists the enabled audit devices in the Vault server. The output lists the enabled audit devices and the options for those devices

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: disables an audit device. Once an audit device is disabled, no future audit logs are dispatched to it. The data associated with the audit device is not affected

states.name: PATH
states.description: Disable the audit device enabled at this path

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: enables an audit device at a given path

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: probes a specific Vault server node for a specified period of time, recording information about the node, its cluster, and its host environment

subcommands.name: kv
subcommands.description: this command has subcommands for interacting with Vault's key-value store. Here are some simple examples, and more detailed examples are available in the subcom

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: deletes the data for the provided version and path in the key-value store. The versioned data will not be fully removed, but marked as deleted and will no longe

subcommands.name: destroy
subcommands.description: permanently removes the specified versions' data from the key-value store. If no key exists at the path, no action is taken

subcommands.name: enable-versioning
subcommands.description: this command turns on versioning for the backend at the provided path

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: retrieves the value from Vault's key-value store at the given key name. If no key exists with that name, an error is returned. If a key exists with that name bu

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists data from Vault's key-value store at the given path

subcommands.name: metadata
subcommands.description: this command has subcommands for interacting with the metadata endpoint in Vault's key-value store. Here are some simple examples, and more detailed examples ar

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: deletes all versions and metadata for the provided key

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: retrieves the metadata from Vault's key-value store at the given key name. If no key exists with that name, an error is returned

subcommands.name: patch
subcommands.description: this command can be used to create a blank key in the key-value store or to update key configuration for a specified key

subcommands.name: put
subcommands.description: this command can be used to create a blank key in the key-value store or to update key configuration for a specified key

subcommands.name: patch
subcommands.description: writes the data to the given path in the key-value store. The data can be of any type

subcommands.name: put
subcommands.description: writes the data to the given path in the key-value store. The data can be of any type

subcommands.name: rollback
subcommands.description: restores a given previous version to the current version at the given path. The value is written as a new version; for instance, if the current version is 5 and

subcommands.name: undelete
subcommands.description: undeletes the data for the provided version and path in the key-value store. This restores the data, allowing it to be returned on get requests

subcommands.name: lease
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for interacting with leases. Users can revoke or renew leases

subcommands.name: lookup
subcommands.description: lookup the lease information of a secret

subcommands.name: renew
subcommands.description: renews the lease on a secret, extending the time that it can be used before it is revoked by Vault

subcommands.name: revoke
subcommands.description: revokes secrets by their lease ID. This command can revoke a single secret or multiple secrets based on a path-matched prefix

subcommands.name: monitor
subcommands.description: stream log messages of a Vault server. The monitor command lets you listen for log levels that may be filtered out of the server logs. For example, the server m

subcommands.name: namespace
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for interacting with Vault namespaces. These subcommands operate in the context of the namespace that the currently logged in to

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: create a child namespace. The namespace created will be relative to the namespace provided in either the VAULT_NAMESPACE environment variable or -namespace CLI

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete an existing namespace. The namespace deleted will be relative to the namespace provided in either the VAULT_NAMESPACE environment variable or -namespace

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists the enabled child namespaces

subcommands.name: lock
subcommands.description: lock the current namespace, and all descendants

subcommands.name: lookup
subcommands.description: get information about the namespace of the locally authenticated token

subcommands.name: unlock
subcommands.description: unlock the current namespace, and all descendants, with unlock key

subcommands.name: operator
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for operators interacting with Vault. Most users will not need to interact with these commands. Here are a few examples of the o

subcommands.name: diagnose
subcommands.description: this command troubleshoots Vault startup issues, such as TLS configuration or auto-unseal. It should be run using the same environment variables and configurati

subcommands.name: generate-root
subcommands.description: generates a new root token by combining a quorum of share holders. Must provide either '-otp' or '-pgp-key'

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: initializes a Vault server. Initialization is the process by which Vault's storage backend is prepared to receive data. Since Vault servers share the same stora

subcommands.name: key-status
subcommands.description: provides information about the active encryption key. Specifically, the current key term and the key installation time

subcommands.name: members
subcommands.description: provides the details of all the nodes in the cluster

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: this command starts a storage backend migration process to copy all data from one backend to another. This operates directly on encrypted data and does not requ

subcommands.name: raft
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for operators interacting with the Vault raft storage backend. Most users will not need to interact with these commands. Here ar

subcommands.name: autopilot
subcommands.description: raft auto pilot

subcommands.name: get-config
subcommands.description: returns the configuration of the autopilot subsystem under integrated storage

subcommands.name: set-config
subcommands.description: modify the configuration of the autopilot subsystem under integrated storage

subcommands.name: state
subcommands.description: displays the state of the raft cluster under integrated storage as seen by autopilot

subcommands.name: join
subcommands.description: joins a node to the Raft cluster

subcommands.name: list-peers
subcommands.description: returns the Raft peer set

subcommands.name: remove-peer
subcommands.description: removes a node from the Raft cluster

subcommands.name: snapshot
subcommands.description: restores and saves snapshots from the Raft cluster

subcommands.name: restore
subcommands.description: installs the provided snapshot, returning the cluster to the state defined in it

subcommands.name: save
subcommands.description: saves a snapshot of the current state of the Raft cluster into a file

subcommands.name: rekey
subcommands.description: generates a new set of unseal keys. This can optionally change the total number of key shares or the required threshold of those key shares to reconstruct the r

states.name: key
states.description: An unseal key may be provided directly on the command line as an argument to the command. If key is specified as '-', the command will read from stdin. If a TTY

subcommands.name: rotate
subcommands.description: rotates the underlying encryption key which is used to secure data written to the storage backend. This installs a new key in the key ring. This new key is used

subcommands.name: seal
subcommands.description: seals the Vault server. Sealing tells the Vault server to stop responding to any operations until it is unsealed. When sealed, the Vault server discards its in-

subcommands.name: step-down
subcommands.description: forces the Vault server at the given address to step down from active duty. While the affected node will have a delay before attempting to acquire the leader lo

subcommands.name: unseal
subcommands.description: provide a portion of the root key to unseal a Vault server. Vault starts in a sealed state. It cannot perform operations until it is unsealed. This command acce

states.name: key
states.description: The unseal key can be supplied as an argument to the command, but this is not recommended as the unseal key will be available in your history

subcommands.name: usage
subcommands.description: list the client counts for the default reporting period

subcommands.name: path-help
subcommands.description: retrieves API help for paths. All endpoints in Vault provide built-in help in markdown format. This includes system paths, secret engines, and auth methods

subcommands.name: plugin
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for interacting with Vault's plugins and the plugin catalog. The plugin catalog is divided into three types: 'auth', 'database',

subcommands.name: deregister
subcommands.description: deregister an existing plugin in the catalog. If the plugin does not exist, no action is taken (the command is idempotent). The argument of type takes 'auth', '

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: displays information about a plugin in the catalog with the given name. If the plugin does not exist, an error is returned. The argument of type takes 'auth', '

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists available plugins registered in the catalog. This does not list whether plugins are in use, but rather just their availability. The last argument of type

subcommands.name: register
subcommands.description: registers a new plugin in the catalog. The plugin binary must exist in Vault's configured plugin directory. The argument of type takes 'auth', 'database', or 's

subcommands.name: reload
subcommands.description: reloads mounted plugins. Either the plugin name or the desired plugin mount(s) must be provided, but not both. In case the plugin name is provided, all of its c

subcommands.name: reload-status
subcommands.description: retrieves the status of a recent cluster plugin reload. The reload id must be provided

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: deletes the policy named NAME in the Vault server. Once the policy is deleted, all tokens associated with the policy are affected immediately

subcommands.name: fmt
subcommands.description: formats a local policy file to the policy specification. This command will overwrite the file at the given PATH with the properly-formatted policy file contents

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists the names of the policies that are installed on the Vault server

subcommands.name: read
subcommands.description: prints the contents and metadata of the Vault policy named NAME. If the policy does not exist, an error is returned

subcommands.name: write
subcommands.description: uploads a policy with name NAME from the contents of a local file PATH or stdin. If PATH is '-', the policy is read from stdin. Otherwise, it is loaded from the

subcommands.name: print
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for interacting with Vault's runtime values

subcommands.name: token
subcommands.description: prints the value of the Vault token that will be used for commands, after taking into account the configured token-helper and the environment

subcommands.name: secrets
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for interacting with Vault's secrets engines. Each secret engine behaves differently. Please see the documentation for more info

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: disables a secrets engine at the given PATH. The argument corresponds to the enabled PATH of the engine, not the TYPE! All secrets created by this engine are re

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: enables a secrets engine. By default, secrets engines are enabled at the path corresponding to their TYPE, but users can customize the path using the -path opti

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists the enabled secret engines on the Vault server. This command also outputs information about the enabled path including configured TTLs and human-friendly

subcommands.name: move
subcommands.description: moves an existing secrets engine to a new path. Any leases from the old secrets engine are revoked, but all configuration associated with the engine is preserve

subcommands.name: tune
subcommands.description: tunes the configuration options for the secrets engine at the given PATH. The argument corresponds to the PATH where the secrets engine is enabled, not the TYPE

subcommands.name: ssh
subcommands.description: establishes an SSH connection with the target machine

subcommands.name: token
subcommands.description: this command groups subcommands for interacting with tokens. Users can create, lookup, renew, and revoke tokens

subcommands.name: capabilities
subcommands.description: fetches the capabilities of a token for a given path. If a TOKEN is provided as an argument, the '/sys/capabilities' endpoint and permission is used. If no TOKE

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: creates a new token that can be used for authentication. This token will be created as a child of the currently authenticated token. The generated token will in

subcommands.name: lookup
subcommands.description: displays information about a token or accessor. If a TOKEN is not provided, the locally authenticated token is use

subcommands.name: renew
subcommands.description: renews a token's lease, extending the amount of time it can be used. If a TOKEN is not provided, the locally authenticated token is used. A token accessor can b

subcommands.name: revoke
subcommands.description: revokes authentication tokens and their children. If a TOKEN is not provided, the locally authenticated token is used. The '-mode' flag can be used to control t

subcommands.name: version-history
subcommands.description: prints the version history of the target Vault server
